profile.behavior: Active foliage gleaner; hops along branches and takes insects from the undersides of needles and leaves, sometimes flashing white tail spots.
field_marks: Small, slim warbler with a fine bill and long, narrow tail.
Adult male shows a black face mask and black neck band with radiating streaks.
Bright yellow throat, breast, and belly; males have bold black flank streaking.
Gray-and-black upperparts with a conspicuous white wing patch or two narrow white wingbars.
Gray-headed females and immatures show a pale white eyering and softer streaking.
Undertail is white at the base with a broad black tail tip, visible in all plumages.
profile.habitat: Dense young conifers, especially spruce and hemlock, in coniferous or mixed forest; also uses dense forest edges, shrubs, and low trees outside the breeding season.

sources.title: Magnolia Warbler Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Magnolia Warbler Life History — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology

findings: No material anatomical issue: the plate depicts one head, one fine pointed bill, two wings, two legs with grasping feet, and one long narrow tail.

The illustrated adult male matches the supplied references and live ID sources: black facial mask and neck band, yellow underparts with radiating black streaks, gray-and-black upperparts, and a broad white wing patch.

All visible text claims verify: Magnolia Warbler / Setophaga magnolia / Parulidae; 11–13 cm length, 16–20 cm wingspan, and 6–15 g weight; habitat, foliage-gleaning behavior, sex/age field marks, and white-based black-tipped tail pattern.
